# Game Pillars

## Our Pillars

[3-5 non-negotiable design values that guide every decision]

### Pillar 1: [Name]

**Statement:** [One sentence]

**What this means:** [2-3 sentences explaining the pillar]

**Examples:**
- ✅ [A design choice that supports this pillar]
- ❌ [A design choice that violates this pillar]

### Pillar 2: [Name]

[Same structure]

### Pillar 3: [Name]

[Same structure]

## Anti-Pillars

[Things your game intentionally does NOT pursue]

- We do NOT pursue: [e.g., photorealism]
- We do NOT pursue: [e.g., competitive PvP balance]
- We do NOT pursue: [e.g., linear narrative]

## Pillar Decision Matrix

When evaluating any feature, design, or art choice, ask:

| Question | Pillar 1 | Pillar 2 | Pillar 3 |
|----------|----------|----------|----------|
| Does this support [Pillar 1]? | Yes / No | — | — |
| Does this support [Pillar 2]? | — | Yes / No | — |
| Does this support [Pillar 3]? | — | — | Yes / No |

**Rule:** A feature should support at least 2 pillars. A feature that supports 0 pillars should be cut. A feature that violates any pillar should be redesigned.

## Example Application

**Feature Proposal:** Add a real-time multiplayer mode

| Pillar | Support? |
|--------|----------|
| Meaningful Choices | ⚠️ (diluted by latency) |
| Emergent Stories | ❌ (scripted multiplayer sessions) |
| Accessible Depth | ❌ (networking complexity) |

**Verdict:** Does not align with pillars. Reject or spin off as separate project.
